𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Forty-Four -- Complete

11QMelchizedek

11Q13

A Qumran Eschatological Commentary -- Cave 11 -- DSS Attested

Columns 1 and 2

The Year of Release for Melchizedek -- He Will Carry Out the Vengeance of Creator Judgments -- He Will Rescue All the Sons of Light From the Hand of Belial -- The Kingdom Will Be to Creator Most High Forever

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

Dead Sea Scrolls -- Cave 11 Qumran -- 11Q13 -- Hebrew Primary

CONCERN LEVEL: SAME AS 4Q246 -- NT appropriation postdates this text

Rooted in Tehillim 82 and Yesha-yahu 61 -- Both in This Collection

YHWH and The Creator only

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT · CRITICAL NOTE

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitute titles

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

11QMELCHIZEDEK -- 11Q13 -- COLUMNS 1 AND 2

11QMELCHIZEDEK -- COLUMN 1 The Jubilee of Melchizedek -- The Year of Release -- He Will Proclaim Release to the Captives -- Melchizedek Will Atone for All the Sons of Light -- The End of Days

CONCERN LEVEL — CONCERN NOTE: THIS TEXT CONTAINS NO FALSE DEITY LANGUAGE AND NO CHRISTIAN CONTENT WHATSOEVER. 11QMelchizedek was written at Qumran and predates the documents of Christianity by at least a generation. The Melchizedek described in this text is a heavenly covenant figure from the ancient Hebrew theological tradition — first appearing in Bereshit 14 as the king-priest of Shalem who blessed Avraham. In this Qumran text he is the heavenly executor of Creator's final judgment on Belial, grounded in Tehillim 82 and Yesha'yahu 61. He is never described as a deity, a saviour, or connected to Jesus in any way. The concern noted here is the same as the concern noted for 4Q246 elsewhere in this collection: the authors of a later non-covenant document borrowed this ancient Melchizedek tradition and attempted to use it to construct a priestly theology around their own figure. That borrowing happened after this text was written, was external to this text, and distorted the original covenant meaning. The appropriation postdates this text by at least a generation. The Melchizedek of 11Q13 belongs to the ancient covenant tradition — not to any later theological system. Read this text as what it is: authentic pre-Christian Qumran covenant eschatology with zero false deity language.

1 Its interpretation for the last days concerns the captives of whom it is written: to proclaim release to the captives.

2 Its interpretation is that he will assign them to the sons of Melchizedek -- for this is the time of the year of favour for Melchizedek.

3 And this is the day of peace concerning which Creator spoke through Yesha'yahu the prophet who said: How beautiful upon the mountains are the feet of the messenger who proclaims peace.

4 The messenger is the one anointed of spirit concerning whom Daniyel spoke: until an anointed one a prince -- the one anointed of spirit is the messenger of good news.

5 And Melchizedek will carry out the vengeance of Creator's judgments from the hand of Belial.

6 And he will rescue all the sons of light from the power of Belial and from the power of all the spirits of his lot.

7 And all the eternal divine beings are with him -- he is the mighty one of the divine beings.

8 This is the day of judgment concerning which Yesha'yahu said: How beautiful upon the mountains are the feet of the messenger who announces peace -- who brings good news.

9 For Melchizedek is the one who will atone for all the sins of the sons of light at the end of the tenth jubilee.

10 And Belial and the spirits of his lot who turned away from the commandments of Creator will be destroyed.

11 The covenant of peace is Melchizedek who will execute the judgments of Creator and deliver all the sons of light from the hand of Belial.

12 And this Melchizedek is the divine being of whom it is written in the psalms of David: Creator stands in the divine assembly -- in the midst of the divine beings he judges.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 11QMelchizedek (11Q13) was found in Cave 11 at Qumran and published by Adam van der Woude in 1965. Three fragmentary columns survive. The text is an eschatological commentary that draws on Vayikra 25 (Jubilee), Devarim 15 (year of release), Tehillim 82, and Yesha'yahu 52 and 61 to construct a portrait of Melchizedek as the heavenly figure who executes final judgment on Belial at the end of the tenth Jubilee. This Melchizedek is a heavenly being -- one of the elohim -- who atones for the sons of light and destroys the lot of Belial. The text predates the a later non-covenant document by at least a generation.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez canonical tradition preserves Tehillim 82 -- Creator stands in the divine assembly, in the midst of the divine beings he judges -- cited directly in verse 12 of Column 1. The Orit Ge'ez also preserves Yesha'yahu 52 and 61 -- how beautiful upon the mountains are the feet of the messenger -- which 11QMelchizedek applies to Melchizedek as the messenger who proclaims release. The theological content of 11QMelchizedek is entirely rooted in the covenant prophetic and poetic tradition that the Orit Ge'ez preserves.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic Vayikra 25:13 -- in this year of Jubilee each of you shall return to his property -- and Devarim 15:2 -- every creditor shall release what he has lent -- provide the Jubilee foundation for Column 1's proclamation of release. The year of favour that Melchizedek presides over is the final eschatological Jubilee -- the tenth Jubilee of the ten-jubilee reckoning -- in which all captives are released and all debts to Belial are cancelled. The covenant Jubilee theology of the Torah is here given its most radical eschatological expression.

[CRITICAL NOTE] The New Testament Hebrews 7 uses Melchizedek as a type for its theology -- citing Bereshit 14 and Tehillim 110 and presenting Melchizedek as greater than the Aaronic priesthood. 11QMelchizedek predates Hebrews and presents a completely different Melchizedek theology: not a priestly type but an eschatological judgment figure. The NT appropriation misrepresents the covenant tradition by stripping Melchizedek of his judgment function and reducing him to a priestly model. This text is preserved here in its authentic pre-NT covenant context.

11QMELCHIZEDEK -- COLUMN 2 The Tenth Jubilee -- Belial Is Judged -- The Sons of Light Are Delivered -- The Anointed One Brings Good News -- Melchizedek Executes the Judgment of Creator -- The Kingdom of Creator Is Established

CONCERN LEVEL — CONCERN NOTE: THIS TEXT CONTAINS NO FALSE DEITY LANGUAGE AND NO CHRISTIAN CONTENT WHATSOEVER. 11QMelchizedek was written at Qumran and predates the documents of Christianity by at least a generation. The Melchizedek described in this text is a heavenly covenant figure from the ancient Hebrew theological tradition — first appearing in Bereshit 14 as the king-priest of Shalem who blessed Avraham. In this Qumran text he is the heavenly executor of Creator's final judgment on Belial, grounded in Tehillim 82 and Yesha'yahu 61. He is never described as a deity, a saviour, or connected to Jesus in any way. The concern noted here is the same as the concern noted for 4Q246 elsewhere in this collection: the authors of a later non-covenant document borrowed this ancient Melchizedek tradition and attempted to use it to construct a priestly theology around their own figure. That borrowing happened after this text was written, was external to this text, and distorted the original covenant meaning. The appropriation postdates this text by at least a generation. The Melchizedek of 11Q13 belongs to the ancient covenant tradition — not to any later theological system. Read this text as what it is: authentic pre-Christian Qumran covenant eschatology with zero false deity language.

1 And the year of favour for Melchizedek -- in the final Jubilee he will proclaim release to all the sons of his lot.

2 And this release will be proclaimed to them to free them from their iniquities and from all their sins.

3 And this will happen in the first week of the Jubilee following the nine Jubilees.

4 And the day of atonement is the end of the tenth Jubilee, in which atonement shall be made for all the sons of light and all the men of the lot of Melchizedek.

5 According to all their deeds -- for this is the time decreed for Melchizedek the Year of Release for Melchizedek.

6 And he will by his strength judge the holy ones of Creator as it is written concerning him in the songs of David: Creator will stand in the assembly of Creator -- among the divine beings he will judge.

7 And concerning him it says: Over it take your seat in the heights -- Creator will judge the peoples.

8 He it is who will keep Belial from rising and all the spirits of his lot.

9 And all the divine beings are with him for he is their Prince -- and the power of Melchizedek is the vengeance of Creator.

10 And he will judge those who are condemned and he will deliver all who are of the lot of Creator from the hand of Belial.

11 For Belial is the adversary of all the sons of light -- but his lot will be destroyed and the sons of light will be delivered.

12 And the kingdom will be to Creator Most High for ever and ever -- blessed be his name above all the divine beings.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 11Q13 Column 2 completes the eschatological judgment scenario introduced in Column 1. Melchizedek's role as the executor of Creator's judgment on Belial is here made explicit: he will judge those who are condemned and deliver all who are of the lot of Creator. The parallel with the Community Rule's Prince of Light -- who contends against the Angel of Darkness -- is direct. Melchizedek in 11QMelchizedek is the named eschatological figure who fulfills the Prince of Light's ultimate function: the destruction of Belial and the deliverance of the sons of light at the end of the tenth Jubilee.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez canonical tradition preserves Daniyel 9:24-27 -- the seventy weeks prophecy -- which is the chronological framework that 11QMelchizedek's ten-Jubilee reckoning parallels. Both texts calculate the end of days in terms of extended Jubilee periods. The Orit Ge'ez also preserves Yesha'yahu 61:1-2 -- the spirit of Creator is upon me because he has anointed me to proclaim good news to the poor, to proclaim the year of the favour of YHWH -- which 11QMelchizedek applies directly to Melchizedek's eschatological proclamation of release.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic Tehillim 82:1 and 7:7-8 are cited directly in Column 2. Creator will stand in the assembly of Creator -- among the divine beings he will judge -- is Tehillim 82:1. Over it take your seat in the heights, Creator will judge the peoples -- is Tehillim 7:7-8. The pesher method of 11QMelchizedek applies these Tehillim texts to Melchizedek: he is the divine being who stands in the divine assembly as Creator's appointed judge. The Masoretic texts are the covenant authority for the heavenly judgment role.

[CRITICAL NOTE] The kingdom will be to Creator Most High forever -- the final doxological statement of 11QMelchizedek is the covenant conclusion that every covenant eschatological text in this collection converges toward: the eternal kingdom of Creator Most High. This statement unites 11QMelchizedek with the Book of Giants, the Pesher Habakkuk, the Community Rule, 4Q246, and Daniyel -- the kingdom that Melchizedek's judgment inaugurates is the same everlasting kingdom that the angel of 4Q246 announces and that the Ancient of Days bestows in Daniyel 7. The covenant eschatological vision is one vision expressed in many texts.

11QMELCHIZEDEK -- COMPLETE

Volume Forty-Four -- The Ancient Covenant Record

𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE KINGDOM WILL BE TO CREATOR MOST HIGH FOREVER AND EVER

BLESSED BE HIS NAME ABOVE ALL THE DIVINE BEINGS

11QMelchizedek Column 2 -- Dead Sea Scrolls 11Q13
